title: June 2 pricing: normalize all multipliers to 1×, keep paid-only gates
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Sets `priceMultiplier: 1` on every model — no retail markup anywhere — and keeps the June 2 access gating. All registry-only; no cost/provider changes.

**Pricing — all multipliers → 1×**
- Every model in text/image/audio/embeddings is now `priceMultiplier: 1`. Net vs main: 39 models drop from 1.5× to 1× (price now equals provider cost).

**Access — paid-only (free Pollen users lose access, paid users unaffected)**
- `claude-fast` (Claude Haiku 4.5), `qwen-coder-large` (Qwen3 Coder Next), `qwen-image` (Qwen Image Plus), `wan-image` (Wan 2.7 Image), `qwen-tts` (Qwen3-TTS Flash)

**Tests**
- Replaced the hardcoded 1.5× markup tests with a single invariant: `price == cost × priceMultiplier` for every model, asserted per cost field (holds at any multiplier).
- Regenerated `effective-prices.snapshot` (price == cost across the board).
- Green: enter `pricing-data` / `aliases` / `effective-prices.snapshot`, gen `model-permissions`.

Merged latest `main` (picks up `minimax-m3` + gemma modelId rename; both already 1×).

gist.headline: Zero markup, pure generation
gist.blurb: We've dropped the retail markup across the board. 39 models are now priced exactly at provider cost, making your Pollen go even further.
gist.summary: Normalized pricing multipliers to 1× across all text, image, audio, and embedding models, dropping the 1.5× markup on 39 models so users pay exactly the provider cost.
gist.impact: Pollen credits now stretch further on 39 models with at-cost pricing, while five premium models (including claude-fast and wan-image) transition to paid-only access.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a friendly shopkeeper, stamping '1x' on price tags or managing a cozy retro cash register surrounded by plants, symbolizing the removal of markups.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny shopkeeper apron, happily stamping '1x' on pixelated price tags in a warm, rustic garden shop. A retro cash register sits on a wooden counter surrounded by blooming code-plants. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
